genre_refs: Art Deco / WPA propaganda poster style; Lincoln Memorial
description: A stylized Art Deco recruitment/propaganda poster rendered as a flat vector illustration in a sepia, gold, cream and dark-navy palette. The central figure is a heroic, monumentalized depiction of the seated Abraham Lincoln statue from the Lincoln Memorial, with Lincoln seated in his throne-like chair flanked by the two fasces-topped columns of the memorial's armrests. Behind him radiate dramatic geometric sunburst rays and stepped Art Deco/skyscraper motifs, with a glowing halo behind his head, giving an almost religious, idealized aura. Across the bottom in bold cream all-caps sans-serif is the slogan 'YOUR NATION CALLS', and beneath it in smaller spaced caps the URL 'APPRENTICESHIP.GOV'. The retro 1930s WPA-poster aesthetic evokes patriotic civic duty and industrial mobilization. Per the tweet ('ANSWER THE CALL'), this is part of the DOL 'Make America Skilled Again' apprenticeship recruitment campaign, urging Americans to enter the skilled trades. Target audience is young and working-age Americans considering apprenticeships/skilled careers, framed as a patriotic national-service calling.
